text: The patient is positioned in a prone jack-knife position on the operating table, preparing for a closed hemorrhoidectomy procedure.
The surgeon performs a dorsal perineal block by injecting a mixture of lidocaine and bupivacaine into three different directions at each site. An Onsler anoscope is then placed in the anal canal.

The surgeon retracts the three o'clock hemorrhoidal pile into the lumen using forceps and performs a V-shaped excision extending up to two centimeters above the dentate line. The vascular pedicle is ligated with a continuous suture, extending to the perineal skin.
The surgeon repeats the v-shaped excision at the eleven o'clock position, followed by coagulation and suturing of the pectin at the seven o'clock position.
The surgeon places a hemostatic sponge intra-anally to control bleeding within the rectal area.
